summary: Set 056 Session 1 (1 of 2) — Audit & design-lock. Surveyed the live shared-doc surfaces and found that the substantive migration was applied out of band in commit e5a3476 ("misc fixes to guidance.") before the audit ran, while session-state.json still showed both sessions not-started. Session 1 therefore became audit-and-ratify: it locked the documentation-authority contract (shared operational facts live in engine-agnostic docs or package metadata; docs/repository-reference.md -> 'Documentation authority and release status' is the canonical home; root engine files carry no independent version history), ratified all four open design questions to their recommended option, and verified e5a3476 is complete and faithful (canonical section present; all three engine files point to it with the version walk removed; AGENTS.md's stale v0.8.0 'Extension versioning' block gone; principle recorded in project-guidance.md; grep finds no live straggler treating an engine file as canonical). Authoritative record: s1-audit-record.md, summarized in spec.md S1 Audit Lock. Cross-provider verification by gemini-2.5-pro returned VERIFIED_WITH_NOTES: all four claim-checks held true and the contract was judged sound; one IMPORTANT note (CLAUDE.md carries richer shared content than AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md) was dispositioned ACKNOWLEDGED/OUT-OF-SCOPE per the set's explicit non-goal (the named sections already point to engine-agnostic canonical docs) and recorded as a follow-on candidate; one nice-to-have (consumer-table header 'ai_router' vs 'ai_router copy') accepted into S2 residual scope. No code, no release. Residual S2 scope: align the consumer-table header across the three engine files, markdown render check, final grep sweep, change-log.

reason.specifics: Session 2 is a thin validation + close pass over a migration that is already committed (align the consumer-table header across the three engine files, run a markdown render check and a final grep sweep, write the change-log, flip the set to complete). Same engine keeps continuity of the audit context; low effort suffices because the substantive work already landed.
